one_line_summary: FAM63B (also known as MINDY2) is a deubiquitinase/ubiquitin ligase family member identified in methylation studies of psychiatric disease and in bioinformatic cancer screens, but lacks direct functional characterization in most abstracts.

molecular_action: scaffolds signaling complexes in neuroendocrine cells
tissues: pancreatic islets, bronchopulmonary and GI neuroendocrine cells, pancreas
one_line_summary: FAM159B is a largely uncharacterized adaptor protein with prominent neuroendocrine expression, implicated as a marker of pancreatic beta cell maturation and expressed in neuroendocrine tumors.

molecular_action: regulatory small subunit of serine palmitoyltransferase (SPTssb/ORMDL-related)
one_line_summary: This gene co-occurs in abstracts with SPTLC1 and the SPT complex, suggesting it may be a small subunit modulating sphingolipid long-chain base chain length; direct functional data are limited.

molecular_action: hydrolyzes chitin or chitin-like substrates (putative chitinase)
one_line_summary: Chi3l7 is a predicted chitinase-family member with no direct functional characterization in the available abstracts; all co-appearing abstracts relate to unrelated topics (chromatin organization, chia seeds, T cell differentiation) with no evidence of a direct role for Chi3l7.

molecular_action: modulates lysophosphatidic acid (LPA) signaling at synapses; regulates cortical excitatory transmission
ko_phenotype: homozygous deficiency causes juvenile epilepsy in mice
neuro_disease: intellectual disability, autism spectrum disorder, epilepsy
one_line_summary: D3Bwg0562e encodes PLPPR4 (PRG-1), a neuron-specific postsynaptic membrane protein that attenuates LPA signaling at glutamatergic synapses; loss causes juvenile epilepsy in mice and de novo variants cause intellectual disability or ASD in humans.

molecular_action: auxiliary subunit of AMPA receptors regulating their assembly and function
ko_phenotype: loss causes impaired AMPAR synaptic transmission and movement disorder (choreoathetosis), epileptic encephalopathy
cerebellar_evidence: highly expressed in cerebellum; cerebellar Purkinje neuron circuits affected
one_line_summary: 6430704M03Rik encodes FRRS1L, an AMPAR auxiliary subunit critical for receptor assembly in the ER; biallelic loss-of-function mutations cause early infantile epileptic encephalopathy with choreoathetosis (DEE37) in humans and the protein is highly expressed in the cerebellum.

molecular_action: interferon-induced transmembrane protein with roles in antiviral defense and cell adhesion
one_line_summary: 6330512M04Rik corresponds to IFITM10, an interferon-induced transmembrane protein family member that is unusual in being interferon-insensitive; it shows altered expression in gastric and colorectal cancer but lacks direct functional oncology studies.
Gm5454 is a predicted gene with no abstracts or functional data available.

molecular_action: represses CREB3/Luman-mediated ER stress transcription; regulates energy metabolism and body composition
one_line_summary: A930001N09Rik encodes CREBRF, a repressor of the ER stress transcription factor CREB3/Luman; the CREBRF R457Q variant in Pacific Islander populations paradoxically increases BMI while protecting against type 2 diabetes.

molecular_action: putative scaffold or structural membrane protein (lipoma fusion partner)
one_line_summary: LHFP (lipoma HMGIC fusion partner) was originally identified as a translocation fusion partner in lipomas and is also rearranged in gliosarcoma; its biochemical function is poorly defined but it may act as a membrane scaffold.

molecular_action: mediates leukocyte transmigration and homotypic cell adhesion via JAM-family interactions
pathways: JAM-family adhesion signaling, immune cell transmigration
cancer_types: lung adenocarcinoma, head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
one_line_summary: AMICA1 (also known as JAML) is a JAM-family adhesion molecule mediating leukocyte transmigration through endothelial barriers; it shows altered expression in lung adenocarcinoma and is a blood-specific mRNA biomarker.

molecular_action: scaffold/pseudokinase that regulates cell morphology and tyrosine kinase signaling via condensate formation
cancer_types: colorectal cancer, biliary atresia (liver fibrosis context)
one_line_summary: D8Ertd82e encodes PRAG1 (PEAK2), a PEAK-family pseudokinase that forms dynamic condensates to regulate cell morphology and growth-adhesion signaling; high expression is associated with poor prognosis in colorectal cancer.

molecular_action: hyaluronidase that degrades hyaluronan ECM; promotes cell migration
tissues: dermis, bone/bone marrow, brain (oligodendrocytes), orbital tissue
pathways: hyaluronan turnover, ECM remodeling, TGF-beta signaling
neural_phenotype: inhibition of OPC maturation into myelinating oligodendrocytes (via HA fragments)
one_line_summary: 9930013L23Rik encodes CEMIP (HYBID), a hyaluronidase that degrades high-molecular-weight hyaluronan to generate pro-inflammatory fragments; elevated CEMIP promotes cancer invasion and metastasis and inhibits CNS remyelination by blocking OPC maturation.
Gm9899 is a predicted gene with no description or abstracts available.

molecular_action: nonclassical MHC class Ib molecule presenting peptides to immune cells
one_line_summary: H2-Q5 is a mouse nonclassical MHC class Ib molecule whose expression has been detected in tumors and immune-privileged sites, potentially functioning analogously to human HLA-G as an immune suppressor.

ko_phenotype: knockout males are infertile (testis-enriched gene screen)
one_line_summary: 1700022P22Rik is a testis-enriched gene whose knockout was identified in a CRISPR screen for male fertility genes; it has also appeared in bioinformatic analyses of T-ALL but lacks direct functional characterization.
9630041A04Rik is an uncharacterized RIKEN gene with no description or abstracts available.
9630013D21Rik is an uncharacterized gene with no description or abstracts available.

molecular_action: orphan major facilitator superfamily transporter with unknown substrate
one_line_summary: Mfsd4 (MFSD4A) is an orphan MFS-superfamily solute carrier with unknown substrate; it was identified as a candidate biomarker for hepatic metastasis in gastric cancer but its transport function and physiological role remain uncharacterized.

molecular_action: olfactory receptor that detects odorant molecules
one_line_summary: Olfr767 is a mouse olfactory receptor with no direct functional characterization; it appears only in genomic/bioinformatic screens and has no established non-olfactory role.
Gm10118 is a predicted gene with no description or abstracts available.

ko_phenotype: associated with intellectual disability and congenital anomalies in chromosome 14q24 deletion patients
neuro_disease: intellectual disability, major depressive disorder (methylation association), schizophrenia (CNV association)
one_line_summary: 3830431G21Rik maps within a rare 14q24.1-q24.3 deletion region associated with mild intellectual disability and congenital anomalies; it also appears in schizophrenia CNV and depression methylation studies but lacks direct functional data.
1700084C01Rik is an uncharacterized RIKEN gene with no abstracts available.

molecular_action: activates ITCH (HECT ubiquitin E3 ligase) by relieving autoinhibition; functions as transmembrane structural protein
one_line_summary: FAM189A2 is a transmembrane protein that acts as a novel activator of the ITCH ubiquitin E3 ligase by relieving its autoinhibition; it is downregulated in breast and lung cancers and functions as a tumor suppressor.
Gm7276 is a predicted gene with no description or abstracts available.
Gm5859 is a predicted gene with no description or abstracts available.

molecular_action: TLDc domain-containing protein; interacts with V-ATPase and provides oxidative stress protection
neuro_disease: neurological disease (TLDc family associated with neurological conditions)
one_line_summary: Gm1332 (likely Tldc1 or Tldc2) encodes a TLDc domain-containing protein that interacts with the proton-pumping V-ATPase and belongs to a family implicated in oxidative stress protection and neurological health.

molecular_action: voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.2; generates action potential upstroke in neurons
pathways: voltage-gated sodium channel signaling, neuronal excitability
ko_phenotype: loss-of-function: ASD, intellectual disability, hypotonia; gain-of-function: early-onset epileptic encephalopathy
neural_phenotype: autism spectrum disorder, epilepsy, intellectual disability, episodic ataxia
cerebellar_evidence: expressed and functionally important in cerebellar circuits; episodic ataxia phenotype
neuro_disease: autism spectrum disorder, epileptic encephalopathy, episodic ataxia
one_line_summary: SCN2A1 encodes Nav1.2, a voltage-gated sodium channel critical for neuronal excitability; de novo loss-of-function mutations cause ASD and intellectual disability while gain-of-function mutations cause early-onset epileptic encephalopathy with severe outcomes.
6330403L08Rik is a putative lncRNA identified as differentially expressed in a mouse model of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, with no direct functional characterization.

molecular_action: T cell receptor alpha chain variable region; mediates antigen recognition
one_line_summary: Trav3-1 encodes a T cell receptor alpha chain variable region segment used in antigen recognition; it appears in TCR repertoire studies of EBV infection and metal allergy but has no independent gene-level function.
Gm4070 is a predicted gene with no description or abstracts available.
Gm12394 is a predicted gene with bioinformatic associations to non-obstructive azoospermia and acral melanoma but no direct functional characterization.

molecular_action: serine/threonine protein kinase C gamma; phosphorylates substrates in PKC signaling
key_substrates: MAPK pathway components, synaptic proteins; activated by DAG and calcium
pathways: PKC signaling, MAPK pathway, calcium signaling
ko_phenotype: SCA14: cerebellar degeneration, ataxia; Prkcc knockout mice show altered postural control
neural_phenotype: spinocerebellar ataxia type 14 (SCA14): cerebellar degeneration, dysarthria, nystagmus
cerebellar_evidence: direct and primary role in Purkinje cell function and cerebellar ataxia
one_line_summary: PRKCC (PKCgamma) is a neuron-enriched serine/threonine kinase predominantly expressed in cerebellar Purkinje cells; gain-of-function mutations cause spinocerebellar ataxia type 14 (SCA14) through toxic PKCgamma aggregation and aberrant signaling.
